changes.value:
## What

`swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/doctor.rs:27-41` `detect_project_type()` returns the first match — Cargo.toml wins and package.json is never checked. In a mixed Rust+TS repo the doctor report says project_type is "rust" only.

Should either return `Vec<String>` of all detected types, or reuse `swissarmyhammer-project-detection::detect_projects()` which already handles multi-project detection with 10+ project types and recursive discovery.

**Affected files:**
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/doctor.rs` — change `detect_project_type` to return multiple types
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/doctor.rs` — update `DoctorReport` struct and `run_doctor()` to aggregate LSP checks across all detected types

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `run_doctor()` on this repo reports both "rust" and "javascript" project types
- [ ] LSP availability checks include both rust-analyzer and typescript-language-server
- [ ] DoctorReport serialization includes all detected project types

## Tests
- [ ] Add test with tempdir containing both Cargo.toml and package.json
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --package swissarmyhammer-tools`
Code-context doctor: return multiple project types instead of first match
